grant_title: #BuffaloSyllabus: Community Healing, and Reparative Justice in the Aftermath of Racial Violence
geographic_restrictions: Primarily focused on Buffalo, New York, with relevance to Western New York and Rust Belt regions
project_area: Racial violence, restorative justice, community repair, Black history, residential segregation, food apartheid, and reparative justice in Buffalo, New York
demographic_requirements: Projects must center Black communities in Buffalo, particularly those affected by racial violence, segregation, and systemic inequities. Priority given to initiatives led by or in partnership with Black Buffalo residents, scholars, and organizations.
eligibility_summary: Open to individuals, organizations, and institutions engaged in research, education, or community-based projects that address racial violence, restorative justice, and reparative justice in Buffalo, New York. Applicants must demonstrate a commitment to Black-led initiatives and community healing. Projects should align with the themes of the #BuffaloSyllabus, including historical context, community repair, and abolitionist frameworks.
